issues.title: Incorrect lifetime_value calculation
issues.problem: The query calculates the lifetime value for customers but does not filter by transaction status 'COMPLETED'.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value

issues.title: Missing filter for total_orders
issues.problem: The query counts all transactions without filtering by status 'COMPLETED'.
issues.fix: COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.transaction_id ELSE NULL END) as total_orders

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which could be helpful for future maintainers.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier, SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value, COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.transaction_id ELSE NULL END) as total_orders FROM dim_customer c LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED' GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000 ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;
review.summary: The query had issues with calculating lifetime value and total orders correctly. It also lacked comments for readability.

issues.title: Incorrect calculation of failure rate
issues.problem: The failure rate is being calculated incorrectly because failed_count is not defined in the SELECT clause but used in the calculation.
issues.fix: Use a subquery or a CASE statement to calculate the failure rate.

issues.title: Potential performance issue with correlated subquery
issues.line: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2 WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED' AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date
issues.problem: The correlated subquery can be inefficient as it runs for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN or a derived table to calculate the failed_count.

issues.title: Lack of comments on complex logic
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, making it harder to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the logic of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
WITH failed_transactions AS (
  SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) as failed_count
  FROM fact_transactions
  WHERE status = 'FAILED'
  GROUP BY transaction_date
)
SELECT t.transaction_date,
       COALESCE(ft.failed_count, 0) as failed_count,
       COUNT(*) as total_count,
       COALESCE(ft.failed_count, 0) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) as failure_rate
FROM fact_transactions t
LEFT JOIN failed_transactions ft ON t.transaction_date = ft.transaction_date
GROUP BY t.transaction_date, COALESCE(ft.failed_count, 0)
ORDER BY t.transaction_date;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with the failure rate calculation and a performance issue with the correlated subquery. Additionally, it lacked comments for readability.

issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: Revenue should only include completed transactions, but all transactions are summed.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as category_revenue

issues.title: Missing comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic and purpose of each clause.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT m.category, t.customer_id, SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as category_revenue, COUNT(*) as transaction_count FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id WHERE t.transaction_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY m.category ORDER BY category_revenue ASC;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with revenue calculation and lacked readability due to missing comments.

issues.title: Incorrect Revenue Calculation
issues.problem: Revenue should only include transactions with status 'COMPLETED'.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_revenue

issues.title: Potential Performance Issue
issues.problem: Using implicit join syntax can lead to less readable and potentially slower queries.
issues.fix: FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Lack of Comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which can make it harder to understand for other developers.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_revenue,
       COUNT(*) as txn_count
FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
WHERE t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10;
review.summary: The query has a critical correctness issue with revenue calculation and a readability issue with lack of comments. It also uses an implicit join which could be replaced with an explicit join for better readability and potentially better performance.
